column2: tasks, UI-TARS-1.5 achieved the highest success rate in both "finding and destroying  specific blocks" and "searching and defeating hostile creatures," particularly when the
"thinking module" was enabled, verifying the effectiveness of the "think-then-act"

mechanism. The success of UI-TARS-1.5 stems from the team's technical exploration across four dimensions: enhanced visual perception, System 2 reasoning mechanism, unified action modeling, and a self-evolving training paradigm. Through these technologies, UI-TARS-1.5 can achieve precise GUI operations and excel in complex tasks. For example, if a user wants the model to "increase the font size," generic models often misunderstand and make mistakes, while UI-TARS can quickly locate the "settings" menu and, based on existing knowledge, reason out the correct path to accurately complete the operation. The UI-TARS team believes that agent evolution is shifting from frameworks to models. UI-TARS is a "native agent model," possessing an integrated perception-reasoning- memory-action structure that continuously accumulates knowledge and experience during training, resulting in stronger generalization and adaptability. This "data-driven" closed-loop paradigm allows UI-TARS to avoid relying on manual rules and prompt engineering, eliminating the need to repeatedly set interaction steps and significantly lowering the development threshold. In the future, the UI-TARS team will continue to enhance UI-TARS's performance in complex tasks through reinforcement learning, aiming to achieve near-human levels. They will also continue to optimize UI-TARS's user experience, further improving usability and enriching its interactive capabilities. The open-source release of UI-TARS-1.5 provides developers and researchers with a powerful tool to advance multi-modal agent technology. Open-source addresses: - GitHub: https://github.com/bytedance/UI-TARS - Website: https://seed-tars.com/ - Arxiv: https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.12326
